The background is softly blurred with warm golden lights and hints of larger airships and clockwork structures, creating a dreamlike workshop atmosphere filled with invention, imagination, and gentle mechanical magic.

images.alt:
A weathered yellow robot kneels alone inside the ruins of a shattered greenhouse during a cold rainstorm. Its body is heavily damaged, with torn panels, exposed machinery, rusted joints, and strands of dark debris hanging from its frame. Water streams down the cracked glass walls and puddles across the floor, reflecting the robot's bent silhouette. The robot's posture is delicate and almost human, crouched low with one hand extended toward a single glowing flower. The bloom radiates a soft golden-white light that stands out against the gray-blue environment. Broken roof panels reveal a pale sky overhead, while fragments of glass and twisted supports frame the scene. Despite the destruction surrounding it, the robot appears captivated by the fragile flower, as though discovering beauty for the first time. The contrast between decaying technology and living nature creates a powerful sense of hope, curiosity, and quiet resilience amid the ruins of a forgotten world.
Keywords:
robot, flower, greenhouse, rain, ruins, post-apocalyptic, hope, technology, decay, resilience, glowing bloom, solitude

denying the value of the human endeavour behind a work of art isn't just a matter of preference. It is an implicit denial of  our capacity to endow existence with meaning through our actions. It is a species of nihilism whose end game is despair. So yes, they are wrong.

#generativeAI #art

value.text: But what about those who don't care about those things? Sure, the creator cares, and plenty of others do; but there are people who don't care about the blood, sweat, and tears that went into some artwork. Are they wrong?

Even if slop and artistic creation are one day indistinguishable, the end product is only part of their value. Two identical works are not of equal value if one was hard-won through years of blood, sweat, and tears, and the other spat out by a quick flicker of transistors.

#generativeAI #art
